perturbation.original_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party (“Recipient”) shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Data that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party (“Recipient”) shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Data to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Data from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data'. It becomes unclear if 'Confidential Data' is the same as 'Confidential Information' as originally defined, potentially narrowing or broadening the scope of protection. The obligation to protect information is now uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the above, the Parties agree that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information and the Recipient shall have no obligation to hold in confidence such information, where such information: (a) Is already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; or (b) Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, officers, directors, or agents; or (c) Is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder; or (d) Is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the disclosing Party; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data.' The contradicted text defines specific exceptions where information is *not* considered 'Confidential Information' and therefore not subject to confidentiality obligations. By changing the term to 'Confidential Data', it introduces ambiguity as to whether those exceptions still apply to 'Confidential Data'. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty, because it's no longer clear whether the exceptions listed in the contradicted text apply, thus creating ambiguity and a potential contradiction under the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: All Proprietary Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Proprietary Information on the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Information'. While seemingly similar, the lack of a clear definition for 'Proprietary Information' introduces ambiguity. Does 'Proprietary Information' have the same attributes and protection as 'Confidential Information'? This leads to uncertainty regarding ownership rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party (“Recipient”) shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Information'. The Contradicted Text specifically refers to 'Confidential Information' and its treatment. Because the Changed Text uses 'Proprietary Information' instead, and it is not defined in the agreement (as stated in the Perturbation Explanation), the Contradicted Text no longer aligns, because it is unclear if the Proprietary information has the same protection and attributes as the Confidential Information. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement now handles these terms differently. This follows the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party’s right to develop independently or acquire products without use of the other Party’s Confidential Information. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information
perturbation.changed_text: The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party’s right to develop independently or acquire products without use of the other Party’s Trade Secrets. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Trade Secrets. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Trade Secrets
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'Confidential Information' with 'Trade Secrets'. 'Trade Secrets' typically has a specific legal definition that differs from general 'Confidential Information'. This creates a conflict because the agreement doesn't specify if it intends to use the legal definition of 'Trade Secrets', leading to uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS ADS and the Second Party (the “Parties”) have an interest in participating in discussions wherein either Party might share information with the other that the disclosing Party considers to be proprietary and confidential to itself (“Confidential Information”); and WHEREAS the Parties agree that Confidential Information of a Party might include, but not be limited to that Party’s: (1) business plans, methods, and practices; (2) personnel, customers, and suppliers; (3) inventions, processes, methods, products, patent applications, and other proprietary rights; or (4) specifications, drawings, sketches, models, samples, tools, computer programs, technical information, or other related information;
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information' which the 'Contradicted Text' defines broadly, including business plans, personnel, inventions, etc. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Trade Secrets'. 'Trade Secrets' has a specific legal definition. This change introduces a contradiction because the 'Contradicted Text' defines 'Confidential Information' in a much broader sense than the typical legal definition of 'Trade Secrets'. Therefore, the Recipient's ability to develop similar products is now limited only by the scope of 'Trade Secrets' instead of the broader 'Confidential Information' initially agreed upon. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the above, the Parties agree that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information and the Recipient shall have no obligation to hold in confidence such information, where such information: (a) Is already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; or (b) Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, officers, directors, or agents; or (c) Is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder; or (d) Is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the disclosing Party; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the above, the Parties agree that intelligence shall not be deemed Confidential Information and the Recipient shall have no obligation to hold in confidence such information, where such information: (a) Is already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; or (b) Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, officers, directors, or agents; or (c) Is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder; or (d) Is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the disclosing Party; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'information' with 'intelligence'. The word 'intelligence' introduces ambiguity. Does 'intelligence' encompass all forms of information? This shift can create confusion about what is exempted from being treated as Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential either by marking it, in the case of written materials, or, in the case of information that is disclosed orally or written materials that are not marked, by notifying the other Party of the proprietary and confidential nature of the information, such notification to be done orally, by e-mail or written correspondence, or via other means of communication as might be appropriate.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'information' to 'intelligence' introduces a potential ambiguity that creates a contradiction. The contradicted text refers to 'Confidential Information' in a general sense, implying all forms of information that the disclosing party marks or identifies as proprietary. However, the changed text exempts 'intelligence' under certain conditions. This raises the question of whether 'intelligence' is a subset of 'information,' and if so, which specific types of information are considered 'intelligence'. This distinction, if not clearly defined, leads to uncertainty about what must be treated as confidential. Therefore, the change introduces an ambiguity (Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction) that creates a strong contradiction with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The requirement to protect Sensitive Data disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Data'. This changes the definition of the data needing protection. If 'Sensitive Data' is not defined elsewhere, it becomes ambiguous whether it includes all types of 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS ADS and the Second Party (the “Parties”) have an interest in participating in discussions wherein either Party might share information with the other that the disclosing Party considers to be proprietary and confidential to itself (“Confidential Information”); and WHEREAS the Parties agree that Confidential Information of a Party might include, but not be limited to that Party’s: (1) business plans, methods, and practices; (2) personnel, customers, and suppliers; (3) inventions, processes, methods, products, patent applications, and other proprietary rights; or (4) specifications, drawings, sketches, models, samples, tools, computer programs, technical information, or other related information;
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'Confidential Information,' which is clearly defined in the 'Contradicted Text' section, including examples like business plans, personnel, inventions, and specifications. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Data,' which is not explicitly defined in the contract and may or may not encompass all the items listed under 'Confidential Information.' This creates ambiguity and a potential contradiction because the obligation to protect data now relies on a term with unclear scope, leading to uncertainty about whether the previously defined types of information are still covered. Therefore, a reasonable reader would see a strong contradiction.
